mappings.evidence_note: Compulsory topic I 'Heat and Gases': temperature, heat capacity, latent heat, kinetic theory, gas laws, thermodynamics. 23 curriculum hours.
Compulsory topic II 'Force and Motion': kinematics, Newton's laws, moment, work/energy/power, gravitation, circular motion, simple harmonic motion. 50 curriculum hours.
Compulsory topic III 'Wave Motion': wave properties, reflection/refraction, diffraction, interference, sound, light, stationary waves. 47 curriculum hours.
Compulsory topic IV 'Electricity and Magnetism': electrostatics, circuits, domestic electricity, electromagnetic effects, magnetic field, AC. 48 curriculum hours.
Compulsory topic V 'Radioactivity and Nuclear Energy': radiation types, decay equations, half-life, nuclear energy, fission/fusion, safety. 16 curriculum hours.
Elective topic VI 'Astronomy and Space Science': celestial observations, Kepler's laws, stellar evolution, cosmology, space exploration. 25 curriculum hours.
Elective topic VII 'Atomic World': Rutherford/Bohr models, photoelectric effect, atomic spectra, X-rays, wave-particle duality, nanotechnology. 25 curriculum hours.
Elective topic VIII 'Energy and Use of Energy': energy forms, transfer, efficiency, power generation, renewable energy, environmental impact. 25 curriculum hours.
Elective topic IX 'Medical Physics': vision correction, hearing, ultrasound, X-ray imaging, CT, MRI, radiotherapy, nuclear medicine. 25 curriculum hours.
Paper 2 covers elective part (2 of 4 topics VI-IX), 20%, 1h.
School-based assessment (SBA) 20%; assesses practical and inquiry skills.
Public exam uses MCQ, short questions, structured questions and essay questions.
Assessment objectives: knowledge/understanding, experimental skills, data handling/application, communication/evaluation.

draft_notes.note: No explicit difficulty taxonomy in source; inferred from question-style descriptions on p.76-78.
Curriculum planning section is implementation guidance, not assessable content.
